title: pecluma ferruginea
intro:
Pecluma ferruginea, also known as the rusty club moss or rusty pecluma, is a type of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America, characterized by its bright green fronds and rusty-red stems.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of pecluma ferruginea are numerous, and it is often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. Pecluma ferruginea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various forms of use in traditional medicine.
